---
title: "Multiscale and Multiphysics Methods"
subtitle: "Cross-Cutting Methods"
date: "2025-01-01"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
---

# Multiscale and Multiphysics Methods

**Track**: Cross-Cutting Methods

Real-world systems span multiple scales in space and time. This textbook covers the mathematical and computational methods for coupling models across scales and physics.

## Table of Contents

1. Scale Separation and Dimensional Analysis
2. Homogenization Theory
3. Averaging and Multiple Scales
4. Heterogeneous Multiscale Method (HMM)
5. Equation-Free Methods
6. Domain Decomposition and Concurrent Coupling
7. Multiphysics Coupling
8. Machine Learning for Multiscale Modeling

---

## Chapter 1: Scale Separation and Dimensional Analysis

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 2: Homogenization Theory

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 3: Averaging and Multiple Scales

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 4: Heterogeneous Multiscale Method (HMM)

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 5: Equation-Free Methods

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 6: Domain Decomposition and Concurrent Coupling

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 7: Multiphysics Coupling

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 8: Machine Learning for Multiscale Modeling

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*
